name: The Unbroken Way The Way of Balance
description:
The Unbroken Way The Way of Balance

*{Lore The Unbroken Way does not speak. It listens. It is the pause between breaths, the space between thoughts, the stillness at the heart of motion. Those who walk this path learn that strength is not in the clenched fist but in the open hand --- and that the greatest power is the power you do not use.

Followers of the Unbroken Way are mediators, guardians, and seekers of equilibrium. They serve not kings or gods but the balance itself. When a tyrant rises, they do not kill --- they redirect. When a village starves, they do not feed --- they teach. When a student falters, they do not punish --- they guide.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, the Unbroken Way is known by many names. In Thepyrgos, it is the Still Point, studied by monks who seek to transcend the body. In Vhasia, it is the Open Gorget, a philosophy of mercy over force. In the Valewood, it is the Root That Does Not Break, a survival technique of the Lethai-al. Wherever warriors seek to master themselves rather than their enemies, the Unbroken Way waits.

quote "The blade that is not drawn cannot be broken. The hand that is not clenched cannot be bound." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Perfect Balance: equilibrium in all things, neither too much nor too little Disciplined Restraint: power held in reserve, strength shown through mercy Clear Perception: seeing without judgment, acting without attachment Protective Service: guarding the innocent, teaching the willing, guiding the lost itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to the Unbroken Way does not bind a creature of claw and fang. Their Thiasos is a focus crystal --- a translucent stone that hums faintly when they are at peace and grows cold when they are in danger. It requires no food, but they must meditate with it for one hour each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Clouded (its hum becomes discordant, imposing --1 die on all Resolve rolls until they spend a scene in silent meditation).

The Codex of the Unbroken Way is never a book of spells. It is a meditation manual --- a collection of koans, breathing exercises, and ethical precepts. It cannot be read in the usual way; the follower must practice each Rite before it becomes legible. Because the Way resists being fixed in ink, the Codex requires upkeep: one hour of practice per week. If neglected, the Rites become faded (treat as Compromised until re-practiced).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Unbroken Way may touch a held weapon or focus. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Melee and +1 die to Insight. The wielder feels a profound calm, as if the universe has paused to watch. After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Unbroken Way.
summary: The Unbroken Way The Way of Balance *{Lore The Unbroken Way does not speak. It listens. It is the pause between breaths, the space between thoughts, the stillness at the heart of motion.

rites.name: Rite of the Sensing Current
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , Materials: A moment of stillness, eyes closed, breath held for one heartbeat. Effect: For the next scene, gain +1 die to all Perception and Insight rolls. You feel the presence of living things within Near range and can sense strong emotions (fear, rage, joy) as faint temperature changes. Push It: Extend the effect to the full session. Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For the next scene, gain +1 die to all Perception and Insight rolls. You feel the presence of living things within Near range and can sense strong emotions (fear, rage, joy) as faint temperature changes. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Gentle Push
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Instant; Near; Yes. Tags: , Materials: An open palm, facing the target. Effect: A wave of invisible pressure pushes one target within Near back one range band (Close Near, or Near Far). The target must test Body + Athletics vs. your Spirit + Lore. On a failure, they are shoved and lose their next Move action. Push It: Push two targets instead of one; mark 1 SB (Spades) as the force ripples visibly through the air. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: A wave of invisible pressure pushes one target within Near back one range band (Close Near, or Near Far). The target must test Body + Athletics vs. your Spirit + Lore. On a failure, they are shoved and lose their next Move action. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Quiet Suggestion
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Instant; Near; Yes. Tags: , Materials: Eye contact with the target, sustained for a full heartbeat. Effect: One willing or unsuspecting target within Near hears a short suggestion (eight words or fewer) that they believe is their own thought. The suggestion cannot be obviously self-destructive or impossible. Test Spirit + Lore vs. Wits + Resolve. Push It: The suggestion lasts until the target completes it (instead of a single action). Mark +1 Obligation (total +2). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: One willing or unsuspecting target within Near hears a short suggestion (eight words or fewer) that they believe is their own thought. The suggestion cannot be obviously self-destructive or impossible. Test Spirit + Lore vs. Wits + Resolve. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Flowing Parry
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , Materials: A weapon in hand, or empty hands held in a defensive posture. Effect: For the rest of the scene, you may Defend against ranged attacks as a reaction. On a success, the attack misses entirely. On a partial, reduce Harm by 1 (minimum 0). You cannot use this Rite while wearing heavy armor. Push It: Extend the protection to one adjacent ally. Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For the rest of the scene, you may Defend against ranged attacks as a reaction. On a success, the attack misses entirely. On a partial, reduce Harm by 1 (minimum 0). You cannot use this Rite while wearing heavy armor. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Flowing Blade
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Instant; Self; No. Tags: Materials: A light blade (one-handed sword, knife, or equivalent), held in a relaxed grip. Effect: Your next melee attack with a light blade gains +2 dice, ignores 1 Armor, and deals +1 Harm to any creature actively using dark or corrupting powers (GM discretion). The blade seems to move before you do. Push It: The attack also disarms the target (they drop one held item). Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Your next melee attack with a light blade gains +2 dice, ignores 1 Armor, and deals +1 Harm to any creature actively using dark or corrupting powers (GM discretion). The blade seems to move before you do. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Still Mind
rites.description: (Master, 12 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , Materials: A moment of silence, eyes closed, breath held at the still point. Effect: For the next scene, all allies within Near gain +1 die to all rolls, and all enemies within Near suffer --1 die to all rolls. You must concentrate (cannot take other actions) or the effect ends at the end of the current exchange. Push It: The effect lasts for the full scene even if you act, but you mark 2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation (total +4) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For the next scene, all allies within Near gain +1 die to all rolls, and all enemies within Near suffer --1 die to all rolls. You must concentrate (cannot take other actions) or the effect ends at the end of the current exchange. \\
